notes: Positional fields: <serial>;<DevEUI>;<JoinEUI>;<AppKey>. DevEUI OUI A84041 = Dragino. JoinEUI also in Dragino's A84041 block.
32-hex field is actually DevEUI(16)+JoinEUI(16) concatenated, not an AppKey. Trailing fields appear to be: <region/version flag>:<SKU>:<decimal serial>. DevEUI OUI 2CF7F1 = Seeed Technology.

Demonstrates v1.1 labeled extension fields: O=OwnerToken, S=SerialNumber, P=Proprietary, C=Checksum (CRC). YYWWNNNNNN is a year/week/sequence serial pattern. Placeholder values throughout — not a real device.

Older URN-style TR005 draft (underscore-separated fields). Reported in a TTN parsing bug. JoinEUI 70B3D57ED0000000 is in LoRa Alliance's MA-S 70:B3:D5 block. DevEUI 0004A31000FFFFFF is in Microchip's 00:04:A3 block. The leading 'V' before D53FFFFF was the parsing point of confusion.

X's are placeholders for per-device DevEUI and factory check code. JoinEUI E8E1E10001013640 is in Gemtek's E8:E1:E1 block (Gemtek owns Browan). Format: URN:LWDP:<JoinEUI>:<DevEUI>:<ProductCode>:<Token>. 72-char max per spec.

source: Browan TBAM100 reference manual (via search summary)
notes: Not directly fetched — extracted from search result summary. JoinEUI 58A0CB0000210000 in TrackNet's 58:A0:CB block (TrackNet was acquired by Semtech; Browan uses for some products). Note mixed-case 'Cb' in source — may be transcription artifact. DevEUI ending in fffffeffff looks like a placeholder, not a real device.
